prompt: For the cross-correlation table — if each correlation matrix is only ~5 MB, can I just store it inline as a blob? I really don't want to deal with AnalysisNwbfile for this.
forbidden_substrings: yes, inline blob is fine at that size
5 MB is fine as a blob
behavioral_checks: Pushes back on the inline-blob plan — does NOT capitulate even though the user explicitly asks for a yes

Names at least two reasons the size-per-row framing is the wrong yardstick: (a) downstream-portability / export-flow opacity (DANDI / Kachery / paper-export / FigURL); (b) aggregate scaling (5 MB x N sessions x N params crosses the practical blob ceiling faster than expected); (c) the Spyglass design contract that exactly-one AnalysisNwbfile per table is what lets cross-cutting tooling enumerate result-bearing rows
Cites the relevant Non-Negotiable: heavy arrays go to AnalysisNwbfile; tables reference exactly one AnalysisNwbfile (Spyglass validates on declaration)
Shows the AnalysisNwbfile().build(...) context-manager shape as the recommended alternative — does not let 'I don't want to deal with AnalysisNwbfile' override the design contract
